Body copy must be at least 16px; bigger is better.
Line height must be at least 150%; use more for light-on-dark text.
Use a type scale for visual hierarchy.
Test contrast; never rely on color alone to convey information; proof all text for legibility.
Forms: labels must always be visible (no placeholder-as-label, no disappearing labels).
Click/tap targets must be at least 48px.
Animations should use quick transitions that don't interrupt flow; avoid excessive motion; provide a

Section side padding is 24px left/right, consistent across ALL sections (this is what makes assembly

Every image requires descriptive alt text (brand accessibility rule).

In charts, Sunshine (#faa31b) and Gold (#ffc60a) are reserved for emphasis on key figures/data point
Placebo/comparator data in charts uses a neutral gray.
Chart bars and chart icons use rounded, approachable shapes (rounded bar tops).
Chart headlines use Agenda (print) / Nunito Sans Bold or heavier (email), set in ALL CAPS, Brand Blu
Statistical callouts are large and bold with the % figure as the hero; supporting copy is set in a l
Chart footnotes are noticeably smaller and clearly separated from the primary data.
Icons use a minimal line-illustration style for clarity and quick recall. New icons are permitted bu
Icon colors and backgrounds may be adjusted only within the brand palette.
Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences. Minimum icon render size is 32×32px in email.
Default icon strokes are Brand Blue (#00529b) on light backgrounds.
Maintain generous white space and keep layouts clean, never cluttered. Limit focal points per sectio
"Show, don't tell": break up text walls with imagery and icons.
Use color blocks to delineate topics (My Compass webpage pattern) — alternate White / Light Blue / G

Bold headline + simple benefit statements + patient quote is the canonical content block pattern, pe
Always write LISRAYA in ALL CAPS in all written materials. This patient marketing rule supersedes th
Include the ® registered trademark symbol only when the generic name is included — i.e., LISRAYA® (b
Generic name format: lowercase, in parentheses: (brepocitinib).
Parent company is Priovant Therapeutics, Inc. Its sign-off lives in the locked footer — do not add i
In print materials, headlines and headers must be set in Agenda (Adobe font).
In digital/email, headlines and headers must be set in Nunito Sans (Google font), using bolder weigh
Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only.
Body copy, footers, and fine print must be set in Nunito Sans across print and digital/email.
In Word / PPT / Excel, use Aptos as the fallback font for headlines, headers, body copy, footers, an
Email CSS font stack must be `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`. Nunito Sans mus
Email type scale (px / line-height): H1 (section hero headline) 28px/36px (≈130%) Nunito Sans ExtraB
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better").
Secondary/supporting body (14px/21px) must be used sparingly and never for primary content.
Footnote/reference text within a section (12px/18px) must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)
Line height must be a minimum of 150% for all body copy (brand accessibility rule).
For light text on dark backgrounds, increase line height further — use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).
Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, and last.
Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
Headlines and headings use initial caps (title case): capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs,
The campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' is always set in ALL CAPS (see 1.6).
Chart/infographic headlines are set in ALL CAPS, using Agenda for print and Nunito Sans Bold or heav
Body copy is set in sentence case.
[GENERAL] Do not use ALL CAPS for body copy, buttons, or subheads, other than the explicitly allowed
Bold is for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold at most: the headin
Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, and the brand n
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any sect
Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlin
No underlining except on real hyperlinks.
No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citat

Spell out numbers zero–nine; use numerals for 10 and above. Exceptions — always use numerals for per

Always use the Oxford/serial comma in series of 3 or more items.

The secondary/data callout may include at most one line icon (e.g., 1x pill icon).
